There are 3 things that I found that really helped my Torques:

1) Plant your setting foot as soon as you completed the set. I found it helps if the set is to your side and a tiny bit infront of you. The height of your set really determines how fast you need to spin (imo), so keep that in mind. A high set means a slower spin, but you also have to be more flexible to lift your leg to that height. A lower set means faster spin.

2) Don't try to swing your leg over as you would for a Mirage or Butterfly. Instead, raise your leg infront of you, turn towards the bag and hop off your setting foot. Let the spin carry your leg over the bag. Try to keep your eyes on the bag as you spin.

3) To keep your setting leg from going over the bag, try to keep it as close to the ground as possible when performing the spin/dex.

To sum it all up, set the bag then plant your setting foot as soon as possible. Raise your dexing leg, hop and spin towards the bag. Don't swing your leg over! Let the spin "carry" your leg over top. Try to keep your setting/hopping foot as close to the ground as possible to keep it from going over the bag.

And remember: Both sides!!

As with most big moves, take your time! Concentrate on starting with a solid clipper, and setting about waist height. The blurry set need not be that high really. The entire trick to the move is executing the paradox almost simultaneously with the first dex. What I mean is, don't do a regular blurry set, and then turn, cause you will have a lot less time for dex #2. While you execute the set, start turning into the set, so all you have to do is a whirl, instead of a pdx whirl. Ex: Start from a solid right foot clipper (left side of your body), blurry set about waist height while turning your hips 45 degrees counter clockwise, then whirl. If you can kick with somebody who is good at this move, or have a video with live sound, listen to how the move sounds. You will get better at the footwork (ie when to plant each foot) by listening to it performed correctly.

It's a real good idea to have your Pdx Whirls dialed, as it makes this trick sooo much easier. The hardest aspect of this trick, in my opinion, is the stepping set. You want to jump off your support leg as soon as you start to set the bag off your clip. Point the knee outwards and plant the setting foot as soon as possible. To make this trick a bit easier for yourself, set the bag about stomach high to give you plenty of time to perform the Pdx Whirl.

I tend to set fairly low for the PDX Whirl, usually at around thigh level. Perform a quick dex with your setting leg, then catch it in the clipper.

The trick to this move (in my opinion) is in the set. Have fun and practice hard :)

Focus on Dimwalk,Paradox Whirl and Symposium Whirl, a whole bunch. Start your pixie set a little lower than waist high, just before your 1st dex foot touches the ground jump from your rooted foot into a whirling motion. In order to make this look good you'll need an impressive pixie set. Try Smear, Dimwalk, Magellen for a stronger pixie set- Both sides!

The easiest furious trick by far.
Start the dex like Symposium Whirl, but after the dex, quickly move your leg, so the bag goes under it again like doing Stepping set.
So, think it like Symposium Whirling Stepping set. After clearing the set deacently the Butterfly will be a piece of cake.
Be sure to make the two first dexes as SET, that's the hardest part. Good luck.

In order for anyone to accomplish this move, they must master 2 elements: a clean pixie set and a smooth and quick double legover. You want your pixie set to be a little bit higher than the waist. Remember to set your pixie clean(you can do this be executing the dex when the bag is at that peak of its ascent and by turning your hips. When you turn your hips, throw your knee with it and your lower leg and foot will following, hopefully avoiding the bag)so that it travels straight up and down. Once the pixie has been done, plant the pixie foot as quick as you can and throw in that double legover. Your double legover has to be nearly perfect for you to get both dexes in time. Try to get the dexes and precise as you can so that the dexes are smooth. Remember to stay over the bag. Practice is also key. With practice will come speed and coordination. With that will come your smog. Good luck=)

Set this one a little higher and a more behind you, than you would for a paradox whirl. Wait for the bag to be a little higher than knee high, to begin the dexterity. Keep your eye on the bag until you have started the dexterity. After your dexterity leg has begun to circle the bag, whip your head around to spot over the other shoulder. Complete your spin, and land the delay like a normal blender.

It helps if you can hit both Blender and Pdx Whirl consistantly.

I find that it helps when the bag is set fairly low (around thigh height - no higher then your waist) and not so much cross-body as you would for a Pdx Whirl. If you set it too far out, you'll miss the bag when you turn to catch it on the Osis.

Do the Whirl as fast as you can and once you've seen your leg go over the bag, turn away from it and complete an Osis.

A strong stepping set is your best asset to this trick. If you are good at both stepping sets and Barfly then this trick isn't as hard as you would think. Step set the bag about waist height, and follow through as you would normally for a Barfly.

It is much better to think of fog as blurry double leg over than as a blur with an extra leg over at the end. Don't overdo it with your set, you want it just above your waist. It helps to think of this move in two parts, first the set, then the dlo. Set slightly above waist height, turn slightly towards the side where you set from, and jump off of your initial support leg. Example: from a left foot clipper, blurry set slightly above your waist height, turn a bit to your right, then jump off of your right foot and do a dlo.

basically in new jersey terms its a jester stall to an inverted super jester that you catch on a toe stall. very nice i like this trick a lot. A key part of this trick is the placement after the clipper delay its gotta be in a comfortable position to get your leg over it takes a lot of practice.
When doing this trick try not to start the dex until the footbag is on its way back down, this cuts down on how much you have to work and puts you in a good position for the finishing delay

As soon as you school an atomic-set, you realize how easy this move actually is. If you begin the dexterity as you start the set, then plant your setting foot as you come around, you'll find you have plenty of time to hop up into the toe-delay. Practice legovers, giving yourself a ton of time in between dexterity and delay. The next illusion you try will seem simple. With enough practice, you can perform the dexterity, and still give yourself time for another move component. This is known as the tapping or atomic set, and it's used in many of the most impressive hi-add footbag freestyle tricks.

I you think the other two tips given say
completely different sings...you're right.
The one does it while the bags raising
the other one while it's falling.
In fact, both tricks are an illusion, but
I'd propose you to do it on the way down
(I don't say it's easier anyway) as this is
what the trick ment to be. If you do it
while the bag's on its way up you in fact
do (or practice) an atomic set. You should
know where you are at with your practice:
If you just hit a mirage and work yourself
through the 2-add list...forget the atomic stuff.
If you're far better, forget the 2-add list and
grab yourself through the intermediate sets,
means atomic and stepping set...

i actually learned this before i learned a reg. mirage. i was tryin to figure out how to do a tappin mirage, and i got the illusion. this helped me figure out the legover, too. and i built all my out dexs off of bein able to do an illusion. so work on this one. its fun, too.

A strong atomic set and double down is a must for Fusion! Legbeater is a really good trick to build up for this trick. Atomic set the bag slightly higher then you would for Legbeater to make up for the extra time needed for the double dexterity at the end.

What really helped alot in learning this move was getting down a pixie-osis. I pixie it in, flip my other foot around in a frigidosis position and hop over the hack with both legs, ending in a yoda. I was able to do the trick every time within three days of trying.

It may seem obvious, but you really should have your Paradons and Pixie (sets) down.

Set the bag up the same way you would for the Paradon (about waist height and a bit crossbody). Do a quick Pixie set, and plant the foot as soon as you can. From there, all you need to do is a Paradon.

Good luck!

Skooling Barfly should help for Dimmiest (Pixie Paradon).
Like Byrin said - after the Pixie set there is just Paradon. It need higher set than Dimwalk, but not too high.

This move is damn hard! I can do a double around the world quite easy and can almost get triple around, I think a way of doing it would get the footbag up high and circle it quick then straight after wrap the leg around but I have never done it and if I did it I would be bloody happy!

The hard part of this trick is that the spin direction changes.
When setting the bag, you don't have to spin all the 360 degrees before dexing. Spin about 270 and dex.
The Torque parts dex has to be twisting. Do it close to your body.
Scooping the Osis nicely is almost impossible. Try to do it as close to your body as possible (again) so it looks something.

Knowing how to do Pdx Mirages really help in understanding how you should begin Pdx Torque. Before doing Pdx Torques, I always go through the Pdx Mirage motion to see how well I can do the dexterity. The higher and faster you can perform the dexterity, the easier this move will feel. I usually set the bag just over waist height and perform the dex as soon as possible. As you bring your leg over, you want to jump off the support leg and begin spinning (using your arms as much as possible helps too) to finish it off as you would a normal Torque. Good luck and both sides!

When doing a Symp Torque, it really helps when you take a small hop while you are setting the bag to help with the initial jump to perform the dexterity. Use your upper body (especially your arms) to help spin. Having experience in other symposium tricks (PS Whirl, Flail, etc) helps since you need good leg strength to pull this one off. Good luck :)

Ironman's tips for Mobius. First of all, the
"without planting" note in the description of this
move is not necessary. You may plant, especially
once you are really comfortable with this move,
you may plant to adjust for a less than perfect
set and it is still a mobius.

*don't rush anything. As with all moves, with a
good set there is plenty of time.

*Watch your set leave your foot. In theory, you
should do this for all moves *especially spinning
moves*.

*Break it into 2 parts -
set and spin STOP!
torque
(in the end it won't look like this but this is how
you should think about it when learning)

In building for this move you want to learn spinning osis and gyro mirage.

It really helps to know how to spin and do Torques (obviously). Getting the timing to perform the Torque after the spin is key to this trick; you want to spin and spot the bag as you are coming around. Use the momentum from your setting leg to help you follow through with the Torque afterwards. The lower you can set the bag, the better this trick looks (that's just my opinion though). Spinning Osis really helped me in learning this trick along with a few Gyro tricks which helped me get used to not planting the setting foot while I spin. Good luck :)

A good way to practice for PS Whirls is to do Symposium Whirls set from your toe. They really help build up your leg strength to do PS Whirls. Having really strong Pdx Whirls helps as well. When setting the bag, set it as you would for a Pdx Whirl except a bit higher to accomodate the time needed to get your dex'ing leg ready to do the Symposium Whirl.

You need to master furious for this trick.
Skool Janiwalker, Sunilwalker and Fury like crazy.
When you can set the bag high enough and Janiwalker feels like just doing a Ripwalk, go for the Barfly instead of Butterfly. Remember quick planting with first dexing leg after the dexes!
Skooling Blurriest should help for the last two dexes.
High and fast set is the key. - Good luck!!!
Comment: I saw a guy do this and I almost passed out, I almost hit it the other day but gees it's just for the crazy people!

I miss FLAILs whenever I set too high. I try
to set from a toe just under waist high and
circle with my ankle, NOT MY LEG. The ending
delay is caught just as my dex-leg is landing.

For all symposium moves, I use my ab muscles to situp in the air. This will bring your legs a little higher. Also, try and bring your legs up on their own if you still have trouble getting symposium height.

I want to add a little bit more about flail. I always do a little 1/2 inch hop before any symposium move because it creates tension in your muscles, which helps create an explosive spring (think of a pogo stick, and our muscle being the spring). Plyometrics is a good way to gain more height off the little hop.

Also, the dex can be done on the way up, or on the way down. I'd recommend practicing it on the way down, so you can add sets in front of it, like pixie flail, atomic flail, etc.

At first, I could not complete the dex for the longest time. It felt very awkward for me (I have no idea why). What I found out that helped me lots was to do a normal Pixie with barefeet, except circle the hack as fast as I could and just stop and plant the foot. I did that a few times to get the feel of the dex, then added the Clipper at the end. Once I got the feeling of doing the move, I slapped on my Rod Lavers and boo ya!

The key part to magellans and other moves that involve the pixie set is in know when to start the first dex. Hesitate a split second before you start the pixie dex. Also when you do a pixie set you'll want to slightly point your toe upward.

Assuming you've learned both pixie set and legover first all you need to focus on is speed. You will want a good pixie set about waist height. Bring your foot to the ground just touch your toes and start the legover. If you keep the bag close to your body practiclly under you it can make for the ideal small swivels on both moves. Try atw if your having problem with your pixie set. once you get it down try hitting it on your flipside.
Very much like the symposium mirage.

After setting the bag, plant the setting foot. I jump with both legs; the setting foot starts the dex immediately while the sympo leg is in the air. As the dex is completed, try bending the sympo leg to keep it 'airborne'. A tip to catching the bag, stay overtop of the bag as you do the dex and just move your sympo foot underneath to where the bag will land. Naturally as you start to come down, so will your sympo leg and it'll absorb the bag.
Set the bag straight up, about shoulder high. Begin your spin with your head. Spot the bag as soon as you begin to come around.

Give yourself some time by setting the bag at least chest high (straight up). Get the spin over with as soon as the bag leaves your foot. Spot the bag as you come around, and tell that dex leg to "get moving". Finish the dex as you complete the spin, and you should have lots of time for the toe delay.
Most importantly, point the upper leg and the knee *straight* down and only move the lower leg. This avoids you from doing a butterfly-similar reverse whirl.

When performing the Rev. Whirl, point your quad (the top of your thigh) towards your support leg while bending your leg as you would when you are finishing the dexterity in a normal Whirl. Doing so will force the rest of your leg to perform the dexterity over the bag. As your leg comes down, hop off the support leg and catch in a clipper delay.

In all Whirling moves, let your ankle do the dex'ing! You have a much more open window then if you use your calf.

And with all moves, practice on both legs!!

To get them cleanly I set the bag up about 8 inches from my clipper delay, using all the strength in my clipper leg I pull it forwards, arcing upwards and then continue circling. As the bag is peaking I am usually at the peak point or there abouts. I try not to do it as an uptime or a downtime move and rather all midtime so it happens smoothly. As the bag begins to decend I always have my foot already in good clipper position (if still somewhat loose ankled) and I aim to have the bag come down in front of just above my ankle on my shin and then you just simply put your clipper foot under it and catch it delicately.

I think the most difficult part for me is not kicking the bag with my foot when I start the upwards motion around the bag. In order to get that clean I find it is easier to stand more over the bag which causes the dexterity to be done deeper on the leg, which is always a good thing, and thus it misses hitting your heel.

The pickup is a fun and relaxing two add move. From a clipper set, move your opposite leg around the bag inside-out and catch the bag on the same toe. I like this move very much and I still use it all the time (don't tell the Big Add Posse). Try doing it with a lower set than you think you can handle. It's a great save after a bad set.

Sidewalk is the easiest stepping move I have tried. It basically taught me how to do the stepping set, so I recommend trying this one first as you learn the set. I find it best to start with a butterfly and keep your upper body weight above your hips. Jump up of your plant leg and backwards at the exact moment you set the bag. Raise your dexing leg to clear the bag as you jump back. Once both your legs land, simply hop forward for the butterfly. This move can be done with a very low set. At www.footbagfreestyle.de there is an excellent single move video of this move.

You need a good and fast fairy set for this one. It's a good thing if you can do pixie set before you try this one. The fairy is a tad harder but the technique is similar. Lean over the bag and set it vertically. I found that a quick motion for the set will allow you to set the bag just as high and you will be able to plant your foot faster. Lift the bag towards your knee. Once the bag has left your foot turn your hip and knee to the inside of tour body. The bag will go straight up only if you separate the 2 components of the set carefully. Set, wait, turn your hips. After the fairy set you do a reverse mirage. I prefer to set the bag lower - about crotch-high - as opposed to a fairy mirage - about waist high.
It helps to do it really fast... and practice

If you have a good duck (quack quack :P) and a strong Pdx Whirl, then Tomahawk shouldn't be too much of a hassle. Set the bag about head height and about as far out as you would for a Pdx Whirl. The real trick for this move (imo) is the dex. Try to spot the bag as it comes down after the duck and immediately start doing the dex. The bag should be falling fast enough that you'll swing your leg right over it. From there it's all a matter of catching the bag with your clip.

You should have PS Whirls and Tomahawks dialed before doing this trick. Once you can do those without too much trouble, then Symp Tomahawk (aka Mullet) won't be so hard. Set the bag as you would for a Tomahawk and as you duck underneath the bag plant your setting foot and raise your initial support leg. From here, it's just like a PS Whirl that was set higher then usual.

You should step set fairly high for this trick, around chest height or so. As soon as you your setting foot plants you want to jump off it again to do the PS Whirl. Some good tricks to learn to build up for this one are Blurry Whirls, PS Whirls and Backside Blurs.

Like a high level college class, this move has several "prerequisite" moves. You should have a consistent paradox torque, so you understand when and how much to "crank" the torque. You may want to try this move pogo style, as it can save you some time in getting the second dex. In order to get a really good set for this move, school your fogs. The height of the set and the timing of the first two dexes for fog feel very similar to Blurry torque. Toy around with how high you blurry set, there is a bigger window than you might expect.

You really want to use your momentum to help with the Symp Torque for this trick. Spin quickly, and as you start to come around plant your setting foot. Quickly spot the bag and then, using your momentum and your arms to help, jump hard off your leg so it passes over the bag and catch it on an Osis. I hit this trick out of the blue, but Little Apple (Spinning Symp Mirage) and Mobius would really help in learning this trick.

Knowing how to do Barfly (obviously) helps with this trick. Set the bag fairly high, and plant the setting foot right away. As soon as you jump off your setting foot, begin the dexs right away. The bag should fall fast enough that you'll dex right over top of it. Knowing how to do Symp Paradons and Symp DoDs would really help in building leg strength for this trick.

Obviously, a good Down Double Down and stepping set is needed for this trick. Step set the bag around waist height and plant that foot as soon as possible. As the bag starts to descend, begin as you would for the DDD.

Parkwalks and Double Over Downs really help in learning this trick.

Pixie set the bag waist high or so and plant your foot as soon as possible. As the bag starts to descend, begin like you would for the DoD.

And remember... Both sides! Even if your flip isn't fast enough, work it! Work it baby! Yeah!!

Set about chest high and pretty tight to your body (feels a little like spinning osis). Begin your Spin as soon as the bag leaves your foot. Get that spin done, so you are not still spinning when you begin your blender. After completing your spin, spot the bag, and execute your blender.

Your Barflys have to be in shape in Spanishfly (Ducking Barfly). Lift your dexing foot before you even see the bag coming down and start dexing as soon as possible. You have to be fast to make it clean.
The set has to be good. Try to set it so you can jsut duck it without moving much, so you know where the bag is coming and in wich ankle.

You'll need a good furious set for this. Skool Janiwalker and Sunilwalker like crazy.
The set hast to be quick and pretty high too. Janiwalker's set height is nothing with Fury.
Jump into the dexes. You'll have to be fast. After you get the set high enough - it doesn't have to be waist high - just twist the Mirage in, but fast.

You should set the bag fairly high for this trick, about chest/head height or so when you are just learning out and if your spins aren't exactly pro material yet. Spot that bag as fast as possible. You can take your "magic hop" while you are spinning or, like me, as you are coming around (spin 180, spot the bag, hop and spin the rest of the way). The hopping is crucial! Some good tricks to build up to this one are PS Whirl and Spinning Pdx Whirl.

This move can be very tricky if done improperly. First of all, you should have some experience doing an outside-in dexterity followed by a toe stall. This is called an illusion. If you can set the bag from a toe, circle it with the opposite foot from the outside-in, and then catch it with the opposite toe, you are 90% of the way to doing clipper set illusion. When you set for the move, don't set it as high as you think you should. The higher you set the bag, the harder this move becomes. Just after the set, jump just slightly towards the side you set the bag from, this makes it easier to come around the bag for this strange feeling dexterity. Bending your leg at the knee and circling the bag using only the ankle can help you get over the bag if you are having trouble. Once you get this move down, you should try the Atomic moves.

For this trick I set the bag up just above knee height and qucikly plant so that my weight shifts back and I hop forwards into the illusion. I often think of it as attacking the illusion, I really focus on getting it deep in the pocket behind my knee. Remember - the deeper the better! It is important to have a smooth circular illusion motion that ends in time to manage a clean opposite foot toe stall.

One thing that helps when you're first learning this move
is to start the move as if you were going to follow through and decided at the last second to stop
That's what helped me
my tip for this trick is FINISH THE GOSH DARN OSIS. if you can catch the bag back there anyway...why not just spin and GET THE EXTRA 2 ADDS. really now...why is this even a trick

I'm gonna have to agree with the first guy. If you have gotten good enough to catch the Osis, just do the spin and get the other 2 adds.

It looks cooler too.

well, the key to this trick is to start the body rake early, just as the whiling x is coming to an end. Again, this isn't a difficult trick, just follow these simple instructions and you should have no trouble pulling it off.

Doing the op-x-body rake shouldn't be too hard, the hard part is doing the the paradox synopsium dex quick enough so that you are really in position for the rake. You should practice doing tuck jumps really quickly and flexibility is also very important.
nice.
You really have to pivot as you duck, Almost like a half spin!

Practice Ducking butterfly and Osis and Far Osis.
Set bag neck height. Swing your head underneth, you can now view the bag drop beside you once it gets towards your waist bail to osis instead your your normal clipper stall. Try Osis>DuckingOsis>
Osis>DuckingOsis repeat.
Practice.

Don't try to swing too much from the hip as in a standing sweep. Instead, learn by extending up to the bag with your toes pointing away from the net. This will make it easier for you to learn and you will put lots of topspin on it.
